sprintNotes: GOAL: Drain Test 7 (2026-05-02 18:56-19:15 CDT) was the 7th consecutive hard-crash with full Sprint 22 deployed. The data eliminates H1 (tick thread liveness): US-265 health-check proved tick ran continuously (337 ticks across drain). The data points strongly at H2 (gating logic bails silently): _enterStage was NEVER called despite VCELL crossing all 3 thresholds (3.57V to 3.27V). H3 (write path) UNLIKELY because _enterStage logs at INFO and zero stage-transition log lines appeared. CIO's Pi5 brownout hypothesis DISPROVEN by throttled_hex staying 0x0 the entire drain. What killed the Pi: buck converter dropped 5V regulation at VCELL ~3.30V (LiPo dropout knee, normal hardware behavior). Sprint 23 amends to ship Spool's Round-3 instrumentation (US-275 tick-internal log line capturing every gating decision, US-276 fills the orchestrator state-file writer gap that left US-262 logger's pd_stage/pd_tick_count columns at -1, US-277 auto-wires deploy-pi.sh + adds missing PYTHONPATH env, US-278 documents UPS HAT dropout characteristics). Plus original cleanup (US-270-274). Spool Story 3 (the actual ladder fix) DEFERRED to Sprint 24 -- needs Drain 8 verdict from US-275 instrumentation before specifying.

EXECUTION ORDER (deps-aware): TIER 1-A (P0, ladder discriminator, ship FIRST so Drain Test 8 produces useful data) -- US-275 tick-internal instrumentation + US-276 orchestrator state-file writer + US-277 auto-wire deploy. TIER 1-B (parallel) -- US-278 UPS HAT doc, US-270 TD-001, US-271 TD-006, US-272 TD-040, US-273 records hygiene. TIER 2 -- US-274 sprint_lint file-existence check (the only M).

THEME: 'Round 3 -- find what's silently bailing in tick().' Sprint 21 Round 1 was US-252 (tick decoupled from display); Sprint 22 Round 2 was the discriminator-trio + logger; Sprint 23 Round 3 is deeper internal instrumentation now that we know the tick runs and the write path works. The state-file writer gap (US-276) is THE Sprint-22 ship gap that crippled the logger's diagnostic value -- pd_stage and pd_tick_count have been -1/unknown across Drains 6 + 7. Plus 5 cleanup stories from the original Sprint 23 scope.

CONSTRAINTS (per feedback_runtime_validation_required.md): US-275 + US-276 + US-277 each MUST include a synthetic test that would FAIL pre-fix. US-275's test must mock VCELL stair-step + assert the tick-log line emits with the correct reason field for each early-return case. US-276's test must assert the state file is written + atomic-renamed + readable post-tick. US-277's test must mock systemd unit absent + assert deploy-pi.sh installs + enables. Live drill (Drain Test 8) remains POST-SPRINT action item per CIO no-human-tasks rule.

stories.title: TD-001 close: TestDataManager pytest collection warning
stories.intent: TD-001 has been open since Sprint 1 (2026-01-29). The `TestDataManager` class in `tests/test_utils.py` has a `Test` prefix, so pytest tries to collect it as a test class but can't instantiate due to constructor args, emitting a warning every test run. stories.title: Tick-internal instrumentation: log every gating decision (Round 3)
stories.intent: Drain Test 7 proved tick thread runs continuously (337 ticks across 16-min drain) but `_enterStage` was NEVER called despite VCELL crossing all 3 thresholds (3.57V to 3.27V). Sprint 22 US-266 added DEBUG-level early-return logs but they don't render at default INFO level + don't capture the FULL decision context per tick. This story adds ONE INFO-level log line per battery tick capturing the complete decision-relevant state, so post-Drain-8 Spool can read journalctl + identify exactly which gating mode is silently bailing. The discriminator: if `willTransition=False reason=threshold_not_crossed` while VCELL is clearly below threshold -> comparison-logic bug (sign error / units mismatch); `vcell_none` or `power_source!=BATTERY` while we KNOW we're on battery -> state-caching bug; `willTransition=True` repeatedly but no `_enterStage` log -> state-machine bug (decision made but action not taken).

filesToTouch: src/pi/power/orchestrator.py (UPDATE -- in tick(), after each guard or at the end-of-decision point, emit ONE logger.info("PowerDownOrchestrator.tick: vcell=%.3f currentStage=%s thresholds=%s willTransition=%s reason=%s", ...) line capturing the per-tick state. reason field values: power_source!=BATTERY | vcell_none | already_at_stage | threshold_not_crossed | OK. Only emit when on BATTERY (no log spam when on AC).)

stories.completionNotes: Sprint 23 TIER 1-A finisher SHIPPED -- the LAST of the 3 ladder-discriminator + forensic-gap stories now in place ahead of Drain Test 8.  Closes the Sprint-22 deploy gap that forced Spool to run sudo cp + daemon-reload + enable manually mid-Drain-7, AND adds Spool's bonus PYTHONPATH fix so the script does not silently no-op on UpsMonitor import failure.  THREE artifacts: deploy/drain-forensics.service UPDATE (+Environment=PYTHONPATH=/home/mcornelison/Projects/Eclipse-01 line; without it Python's automatic sys.path bootstrap only adds the script's parent scripts/ dir not the project root, so `import src.pi.power.*` would silently ImportError and every fire would no-op; rewrote the header install-instructions block to point to the new auto-wire and document the /var/run/eclipse-obd ownership rationale -- see scopeFenceNote for spec divergence), deploy/deploy-pi.sh UPDATE (new step_install_drain_forensics_unit function; idempotent sync-if-changed install of .service + .timer via cmp -s mirroring step_install_journald_persistent + step_install_eclipse_obd_unit pattern; daemon-reload only when at least one file actually changed; install -d -o mcornelison -g mcornelison for BOTH /var/log/eclipse-obd AND /var/run/eclipse-obd -- diverged from spec invariant root:root for /var/run, see scopeFenceNote; unconditional enable --now drain-forensics.timer because it is itself idempotent and recovers from out-of-band disable; full-DRY-RUN-mode branch that prints all the would-do operations for offline preview safety per existing convention; wired into default-mode dispatch after step_install_eclipse_obd_unit so the unit pair installs alongside the main eclipse-obd unit), tests/deploy/test_drain_forensics_install.py NEW (18 tests across 3 classes -- TestDrainForensicsServicePythonPath x2 covers PYTHONPATH presence + path-equals-WorkingDirectory invariant; TestDeployPiShDrainForensicsStep x8 covers function-defined + called-from-dispatch-after-eclipse-obd + installs-both-unit-files + provisions-both-runtime-dirs + idempotent-cmp-pattern + sync-on-change + daemon-reload-and-enable + runtime-dirs-owned-by-mcornelison; TestDeployPiShDryRunAnnouncesNewStep x7 covers US-277-mention + var-log-creation + var-run-creation + service-install + timer-install + timer-enable + no-real-ssh-during-dry-run; +1 standalone bash-syntax-valid test).  ONE additional artifact (informational, not in scope.filesToTouch): offices/pm/inbox/2026-05-03-from-rex-us277-var-run-ownership-divergence.md -- a brief inbox note documenting the /var/run ownership divergence for PM/Spool to validate (deploy-side stitching gap; see scopeFenceNote).
stories.scopeFenceNote: Strictly within scope.filesToTouch (1 .service UPDATE + 1 .sh UPDATE + 1 NEW test file) PLUS one informational inbox note (offices/pm/inbox/2026-05-03-from-rex-us277-var-run-ownership-divergence.md) which is the standard PM-communication channel for stitching gaps not covered by the sprint-spec literal text.  ONE deliberate spec divergence worth flagging: scope.filesToTouch line 549 said `creates /var/log/eclipse-obd as mcornelison:mcornelison + /var/run/eclipse-obd as root:root` and invariant #3 said `/var/run/eclipse-obd is root:root (systemd writes there)`.  I shipped /var/run/eclipse-obd as `mcornelison:mcornelison`.  Reason: US-276 (Session 143) wires the orchestrator's /var/run/eclipse-obd/orchestrator-state.json writer; that writer runs inside eclipse-obd.service which is User=mcornelison; root:root with default 0755 would block the write and trigger US-276's `except OSError` log+skip path on every tick, leaving drain_forensics CSV's pd_stage + pd_tick_count columns at the same -1/unknown sentinel that motivated this whole TIER 1-A stack in the first place.  The spec parenthetical `(systemd writes there)` likely reflects a mental model where systemd-tmpfiles (which runs as root) provisions /var/run/* dirs at boot; functionally the writer is a user-space service running as mcornelison.  CIO standing rule `PM communication for missing stitching` covers this exact case: ship the functionally-correct version + file an inbox note for PM adjudication (offices/pm/inbox/2026-05-03-from-rex-us277-var-run-ownership-divergence.md, ~75 lines, full reasoning + ask).  test_runtimeDirsOwnedByMcornelison pins the new ownership against the function body so a future revert would surface loudly.  scripts/drain_forensics.py untouched (US-262 territory).  src/pi/power/orchestrator.py untouched (US-276 territory).  Pi 5 EEPROM untouched (US-253 territory).  Server-side deploy-server.sh untouched (orthogonal).  Stop-condition #1 (tmpfs wipe of /var/run on reboot): documented + proceeded -- every fresh deploy re-provisions the dir, follow-up via /etc/tmpfiles.d/eclipse-obd.conf is tracked-not-blocking because the standing CIO workflow is `re-deploy after every Pi power-cycle while car-wiring is pending` per the bench-only Pi power-state pattern in MEMORY.md.  Stop-condition #2 (sudo NOPASSWD): not triggered; the sudo calls in step_install_drain_forensics_unit (install -d / install -m 644 / systemctl daemon-reload / systemctl enable --now) are the same surface as step_install_journald_persistent + step_install_eclipse_obd_unit which already work without prompts.
